name: Cù Sìth
description: The Cù Sìth (pronounced Coo Shee) is a mythical, spectral hound said to haunt the Scottish Highlands. Described as being roughly the size of a calf or young bull, it is known for its shaggy, dark green fur. Legend claims its howl can be heard for miles, a terrifying sound that serves as a warning to those nearby. Some tales say that if you hear the Cù Sìth's howl, you must reach safety by the third bark, or you will be overcome by its power. The creature is often associated with death and the fairy world, and it is sometimes seen as a harbinger of doom. It is believed the Cù Sìth hunts alone or in pairs, and its purpose remains shrouded in mystery, though many believe it guards the fairy realm or seeks out lost souls. The creature is a prominent figure in Highland folklore, embodying the wild and untamed nature of the Scottish landscape and the enduring power of ancient beliefs. Stories of the Cù Sìth continue to be told, serving as a reminder of the hidden dangers and mystical forces that are said to dwell within the Highlands.
subtitle: The Fairy Dog of the Highlands
appearance: The Cù Sìth is a large, spectral dog, often described as being the size of a calf. Its most distinctive feature is its shaggy, dark green fur. It has large paws, sometimes depicted as being disproportionately large, and piercing eyes. Its tail is often described as being long and curled. Its overall appearance is both imposing and unsettling, reflecting its connection to the supernatural.
habitat: The Cù Sìth is said to inhabit the remote and wild areas of the Scottish Highlands, particularly in forests, glens, and moorlands. It is often associated with areas near fairy mounds and other places believed to be gateways to the fairy realm.
behavior: The Cù Sìth is a solitary creature, typically hunting alone or in pairs. It is said to be a skilled tracker and hunter, able to move silently and swiftly through the landscape. Its howl is a warning, either to ward off intruders or to signal its presence. It is considered dangerous and unpredictable, and encounters with the Cù Sìth are often viewed as ominous.

keyMyths.title: The Warning Howl
keyMyths.description: The Cù Sìth's howl is a warning of impending doom. Legend says you must find shelter before the third howl, or you will be taken by the creature.

sources.author: W.Y. Evans Wentz
sources.description: An anthropological study of Celtic folklore and beliefs.
